meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances
meta.deliverables: Monitoring progress on SCDPM Portal; Data Analytics of data received on SCDPM Portal; Drafting Agenda for Monthly Review Meetings of all Ministries/Departments by DARPG; Preparation of Presentations or reports on the update and progress of the Ministries/Departments on SCDPM; Analysis of Best practices from Ministries/Departments on Monthly basis; Preparation of monthly report of Secretariat Reforms on the basis of monthly data on SCDPM Portal; Other work assigned by DARPG.

content.full_text: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions has issued a work order to Quality Council of India (QCI) for extension of the SCDPM PMU from 11.08.2023 to 10.02.2024. The project involves monitoring progress on the SCDPM Portal, data analytics, drafting agendas for monthly review meetings, preparing presentations and reports, analyzing best practices, and preparing monthly reports on Secretariat Reforms. The PMU team will comprise 3 QCI professionals working for 6 months. Financials include Rs 27,00,000 for QCI professionals and Rs 3,24,600 for administration cost, totaling Rs 30,24,600 plus GST. Payment terms are 25% on award, 25% after 3 months, and 50% on completion. Services may be discontinued anytime if unsatisfactory.
